title: IL-15 armored CAR T cells show enhanced persistence and antitumor activity in solid tumor models
abstract: Armored CAR T cells engineered to express IL-15 showed improved proliferation, survival, persistence, and cytotoxic activity in preclinical solid tumor models, with possible safety considerations from cytokine exposure. Low antigen settings were discussed as a challenge for CAR T activation.

title: IL-12 secreting CAR T cells remodel suppressive tumor microenvironments
abstract: IL-12 secreting CAR T cells can increase IFN-gamma and remodel immunosuppressive tumor microenvironments in preclinical models. Reports also describe toxicity and inflammatory risk, so translation requires careful validation.

title: IL-7 support promotes T cell survival and memory-like persistence
abstract: IL-7 signaling supports T cell survival and memory phenotype maintenance. Direct evidence for GPC3 CAR T cells in low antigen density liver cancer is limited.

title: IL-2 promotes T cell proliferation but may increase activation-induced cell death and exhaustion
abstract: IL-2 can expand activated T cells and has a long history in adoptive cell therapy support. High stimulation can increase activation-induced cell death, terminal differentiation, and regulatory or toxic inflammatory effects.

title: IL-18 armored CAR T cells enhance inflammatory cytokine production in solid tumor models
abstract: IL-18 armored CAR T strategies may increase IFN-gamma production and innate immune activation in solid tumors, but context dependence and inflammatory risk remain important uncertainties.

title: GPC3 targeted CAR T cells for hepatocellular carcinoma face antigen heterogeneity challenges
abstract: GPC3 is a candidate target antigen in hepatocellular carcinoma. Antigen heterogeneity and low antigen density can reduce CAR T activation and killing, motivating engineering strategies that improve persistence or activation thresholds.

title: PhysiCell agent-based modeling of CAR T cells in tumor microenvironments
abstract: PhysiCell and BioFVM can represent discrete tumor and immune agents with diffusive microenvironment fields, supporting in silico exploration of CAR T killing, proliferation, exhaustion, oxygen, and suppressive signals.
